Comment: Quadplane -- four lift motors, a pusher and four control surfaces -- on a RealFlight custom model. The "px4" values are HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS.controls[] indices and MUST match PWM_MAIN_FUNC* in ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1202_flightaxis_quadplane (and HIL_ACT_FUNC* in the .hil twin). DESIGN RULE: this table is a PURE IDENTITY PIPE for all twelve channels - rf N == px4 N, RealFlight channel N+1 is driven by PX4 output channel N+1, with no renumbering and no reversal anywhere. WHAT each channel carries is decided ENTIRELY by PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> / HIL_ACT_FUNC<N>, which are chosen so that controls[] comes out in the RealFlight model's own channel order. If the wrong thing comes out on a RealFlight channel, change the FUNC parameter - do not re-scramble this table.
ScaleComment: The "scale" column is the ONE thing here that is not free configuration, because it is a signal-domain conversion rather than a preference. PWMSim emits non-reversible Motor functions as [0,1] and EVERYTHING else as [-1,1] (PWMSim.cpp:77-88), while RealFlight wants [0,1] on every channel. So "unipolar" (pass through) is correct if and only if you assigned a Motor to that PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>, and "bipolar" ((v+1)/2) is correct for servos, control surfaces and peripherals. Get it wrong and the failure is quiet: a motor read as bipolar idles at half throttle and never stops, and a surface read as unipolar loses its entire negative half and only ever deflects one way. RULE: change "scale" on a row whenever you change that channel's FUNC between a Motor and anything else. This airframe is the clearest illustration - rf2 and rf4-7 are motors (unipolar, disarm 0.0) while rf0, rf1, rf3 and rf8 are surfaces (bipolar).
ReverseComment: There is deliberately no "reverse": true anywhere in this file. Direction is not a property of the pipe, it belongs to whoever configures the aircraft, and there are two places to set it - both outside this file. (1) PX4 side: the PWM_MAIN_REV parameter is a bitmask over output channels, bit N-1 for channel N, and it IS honoured in SITL - it is applied in MixingOutput::output_limit_calc_single (mixer_module.cpp:531) which runs upstream of PWMSim, so it reaches RealFlight. Reverse channel 2 with 'param set PWM_MAIN_REV 2'; channel 9 is bit 8, i.e. 256. (Note PWM_MAIN_MIN/MAX are a different story - PWMSim overwrites them with setAllMinValues/setAllMaxValues, so per-channel endpoint trimming does NOT work in SITL.) (2) RealFlight side: each servo has its own direction in the model's servo setup. SYMPTOM of a wrong direction: the surface moves the correct amount but the wrong way, so the aircraft diverges instead of correcting - a reversed elevator pitches down when PX4 commands nose-up. Reversing a MOTOR channel is almost never what you want - it would make the disarmed 0.0 come out as full throttle.
AileronComment: rf8 (RF ch9) now has a row like every other channel, and the shipped airframe puts PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 = 202 (Servo 2) there - the RIGHT aileron of the split-aileron pair the airframe declares (CA_SV_CS0 left -> controls[0], CA_SV_CS1 right -> controls[8]). The two allocator outputs are EXACT MIRRORS: measured in SITL after a transition to fixed wing, actuator_servos.control was [0.45138, -0.45138, ...] and stayed an exact negation at every sample, and CS0 already swings the FULL +-1 range (PX4's pseudo-inverse of the 1x2 roll row [-0.5, +0.5] is [-1; +1]). So on a RealFlight model with ONE combined aileron channel you do not need ch9 at all - that is also what ArduPlane does by default (SERVO1_FUNCTION = k_aileron, one combined aileron on ch1). IF YOUR RF MODEL USES ch9 FOR SOMETHING ELSE, a full-amplitude roll-correlated signal would drive that instead: set 'param set-default PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 0' in the airframe (or assign the function your model actually has there) and the channel returns to a steady neutral 0.5. If your model really is split-aileron, check that the two surfaces move in OPPOSITE directions and reverse ch9 with PWM_MAIN_REV bit 8 (value 256) if they do not.
AuxComment: rf9-rf11 (RF ch10-ch12) are mapped but carry no function in the shipped airframe: PWM_MAIN_FUNC10 and up are unset, so PX4 emits 0.0 on those outputs and the bipolar conversion turns that into a steady 0.5 - neutral, and byte-identical to the UnmappedDefault these channels used to fall back on. They are listed anyway so that using one takes a parameter change and nothing else: set PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> to Flaps, Landing_Gear, a Servo, a Peripheral via Actuator Set, or anything else PX4 offers, and it appears on RealFlight channel N with no edit to this file. Remember the scale rule above if what you assign is a Motor.
OptionsComment: Do NOT add "Rev4Servos": it swaps rf0-3 with rf4-7 wholesale. The airframe deliberately places the control surfaces on controls[0..3] and the four lift motors on controls[4..7] so that they line up with the RealFlight model's channel order, so enabling the swap would drive the control surfaces with lift-motor throttles and the lift motors with surface deflections.
Rev4ServosComment: If Rev4Servos is ever enabled on some other model, give every row it covers an explicit "disarm" rather than relying on the -1 "hold last" default, and get_FAbridge_params.py will insist on it. The bridge itself is no longer vulnerable: buildChannels() applies the swap to a scratch copy and writes only out[], so the persistent channels[] keeps untransformed values and a held slot genuinely holds. When the swap was applied in place on channels[] every frame, a held slot was re-swapped instead of held. Being its own inverse it did not diverge the way HeliDemix did; instead a held channel ping-ponged between rf i and rf i+4 on alternate frames, a full-amplitude servo buzz at frame rate (~250 Hz). Armed, fully-mapped rows were unaffected because the fresh write each frame dominates, and unmapped slots are re-seeded from UnmappedDefault, so only hold-last rows were ever exposed.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: fwd throttle   <- controls[2]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC3 = 105 Motor 5 pusher, already [0,1]
aileron right  <- controls[8]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 = 202 Servo 2 - see AileronComment
unassigned     <- controls[9]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC10 to use
unassigned     <- controls[10] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC11 to use
unassigned     <- controls[11] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC12 to use
